name: Johnny Dodds
description: American jazz clarinetist and alto saxophonist (1892–1940)
abstract: Johnny Dodds (; April 12, 1892 – August 8, 1940) was an American jazz clarinetist and alto saxophonist based in New Orleans, best known for his recordings under his own name and with bands such as those of Joe "King" Oliver, Jelly Roll Morton, Lovie Austin and Louis Armstrong. Dodds was the older brother of drummer Warren "Baby" Dodds, o…

about_quotes.speaker: Geoff Bull
about_quotes.evidence: we had a lot of the old reissues of the Johnny Dodds and King Olivers and the blues singers and Tommy Ladner and all that sort of stuff that they reissued.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Lovett
about_quotes.evidence: No, I think I was mainly trying to emulate the sound of Johnny Dodds. ... I never met him or I never saw him or heard him in person.

about_quotes.speaker: Denise Morel
about_quotes.evidence: Johnny Dodds, I don't think, but there are only some fans, some specialists who they know.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: And back in the 20s when they had Johnny Dodds and Louis Armstrong... were black artists.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Lovie Austin
about_quotes.evidence: Well, Johnny played clarinet with me after my clarinet player died.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Muranyi
about_quotes.evidence: You’re trying to imitate Johnny Dodds. I mean I didn’t — I liked Benny Goodman but I didn’t aspire to that kind of clarinet that I wanted to play. But Johnny Dodds was my hero for a long time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Muranyi interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007
